hq: Not disclosed (no physical office; fully cloud-based)
signing_authority: Not filled in (template placeholder left blank)

system_description.overview: Penguin AI Healthcare Platform is a comprehensive solution designed for Health Insurance Payers, Providers and Hospital Systems to streamline complex administrative workflows and automate repetitive tasks. The platform provides AI-automated compliance management, AI-powered security features including code scanning and infrastructure monitoring, and compliance support services.

products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS; integration with client technology stacks; continuous compliance monitoring and gap identification; automated evidence collection
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning for security vulnerabilities; automated infrastructure scanning; access control monitoring; security questionnaire automation
products.Compliance Support Services: Onboarding, setup, framework implementation; ongoing compliance expert guidance and auditor coordination

infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices or servers; fully cloud-based on AWS
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (report states 'high availability data centers with multiple availability zones')

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs); customer accounts are appropriately segmented from each other
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services; reviewed annually
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access
network_architecture.tls: TLS (version not specified) with certificates from established third-party CA for all web-based communications
network_architecture.content_filtering: Proxy server with content filtering for endpoint internet access
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic enters through Internet Gateway into VPC environment; segmented VPCs with private subnets for production, dev, and test; VPN gateway for remote admin access; IDS/IPS via AWS; bastion host for administrative remote access

application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test, and production environments

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policy exists; frequency not specified

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (password policy via AWS; Active Directory referenced for server/DB accounts)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; 2FA required for email access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements, account lockout after limited failed attempts; managed via AWS password policy

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS; access to production keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (generic 'source code management tool' referenced)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured; every merge request to production requires additional approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as code; configuration management tool used for rollback (tool not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations auto-update and scanned daily for patch compliance; critical/security OS patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; IT management notified

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, data center services, hosting

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution installed on all desktops and laptops; anti-virus on production servers with continuous scanning; definitions updated daily (workstations) and weekly (production servers)
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan before every production release
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to facilities; AWS is responsible for physical security of data centers
A1.2 — Environmental protections/backup infrastructure; Penguin AI has no office, managed by AWS
notable_controls: MFA enforced for admin and source code tool access (CC6.1.1, CC8.1.3)
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged (CC6.2.2)
Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation tracking (CC7.1.2)
Annual external penetration testing with high/critical findings tracked to remediation (CC4.1)
BCDR plan tested annually against multiple loss scenarios (CC7.4.1, CC9.1)
Source code changes require MFA and branch approval (CC8.1.2, CC8.1.3)
Vendor risk assessment quarterly for critical vendors without SOC reports (CC9.2.1)

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with multi-AZ, VPC, IDS/IPS, and bastion host. No specific regions named, no multi-region, no CDN mentioned, no named IaC tool. Network diagram in report is a placeholder/sample, not actual.

application_architecture.rationale: No programming languages, frameworks, or CI/CD tools named. Separate environments and branch protection confirmed. Application architecture pattern not disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: No specific database, caching, or storage technologies named. Backup policy exists but frequency not specified. Data classification and retention policies confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong: MFA on admin/source code, annual external pentest, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection, firewalls, quarterly access reviews. Weak: WAF not mentioned, no named SIEM, network diagram is a template placeholder.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection and separate environments confirmed, IaC referenced but no tool named, CI/CD tool unnamed, source control unnamed. Monthly patch reviews and automatic patching are positives.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, reviewed and tested annually, multi-AZ failover via AWS. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not specified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a subservice organization. No other vendors explicitly named. High single-cloud concentration with no disclosed redundancy providers.

overall.rationale: The report shows a foundational security posture with key controls in place (MFA, pentest, vuln scanning, BCDR). However, significant gaps in technical specificity — no named CI/CD, source control, databases, languages, or monitoring tools. The network diagram is a template placeholder, which is a significant red flag for report quality. The management assertion signature block was also left blank (template not completed).

red_flags: Network diagram on page 19 is explicitly a sample/placeholder ('Please replace this with your actual network diagram') — Penguin AI did not provide their actual network topology
Management assertion signing authority name and designation left blank (template not completed) — raises questions about report completeness and oversight
No CI/CD tool, source control system, or programming languages named anywhere in the report
No database or storage technologies specified
Only one named vendor/subservice organization (AWS) — no other critical third-party tools disclosed
yellow_flags: No WAF explicitly mentioned despite being a healthcare-adjacent compliance platform
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite BCDR testing being confirmed
No SIEM tool named; monitoring described generically
Backup frequency not specified in the report
Report uses an Accorp Partners template with yellow-highlighted instruction text still visible in the system description section — suggests minimal customization
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation tracking
MFA enforced for admin access and source code tool access
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revocation within 1 business day of termination
BCDR plan tested annually against multiple loss scenarios
Separate development, test, and production environments
All controls tested with no exceptions noted (clean SOC 2 Type 2 opinion)
key_observations: This report was produced using the Accorp Partners SOC 2 template and retains visible template placeholders including a sample network diagram and unfilled signing authority — suggests the company may be going through their first SOC 2 and did not fully customize the report
Penguin AI is itself a compliance automation platform (sells SOC 2 automation) yet their own report lacks the technical specificity expected — a notable irony that warrants deeper diligence
Trust criteria scope includes Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy — broad scope for a first audit period (only 3 months: Sept-Dec 2025)
Healthcare-adjacent platform (Health Insurance Payers, Providers, Hospital Systems) but no HIPAA mention in the audit scope itself — only in the product description
No named monitoring tool, SIEM, or log aggregation platform is a gap for a company that sells infrastructure monitoring as a product feature
